Write the voice-over as a continuous, chronological story.

Rules:
- Follow the timestamps strictly. Never describe a later moment before an earlier one.
- Ground every claim in the digest. If the digest does not say a train is present, do not put one there.
- Name stations only when the on-screen text names them.
- Vary the language; do not repeat the same sentence shape.
- Write one section per title card, in order. Begin each section with a markdown heading naming that line exactly, e.g. '## Bakerloo Line'. Mention the timestamp of at least one moment in each section as m:ss.
- No preamble, no closing commentary. Begin with the first heading.
response:
## Bakerloo Line
At 0:09, we begin our journey at Embankment. A woman makes her way down the platform, passing a crowd of commuters, before a train arrives to collect passengers at Castle.

## Central Line
Moving to the Central Line, we find ourselves at Bethnal Green at 2:21. Soon, the rhythmic hum of the city returns as a train descends the tracks into the station.

## Circle Line
By 4:41, the view shifts to Temple. Here, we observe the steady flow of the network as a train pulls passengers along the rails.

## District Line
At 5:52, a sign marks our arrival at Stepney Green. The platforms are bustling, filled with a dense throng of people waiting as the train pulls into the station.

## Hammersmith City Line
Returning once more to Stepney Green at 8:57, we transition to the Hammersmith City Line. A train arrives, boarding a fresh group of travelers.

## Jubilee Line
At 10:13, we stop at Cottage. The pace slows as a subway train comes to a complete halt at the station platform.

## Metropolitan Line
We reach Finchley Road at 12:26. On the southbound platform, a massive surge of passengers boards the Metropolitan line, filling the carriages to capacity.

## Northern Line
At 15:00, we see a sign on the wall before arriving at Charing Cross, where a man sits on the station floor. By 17:01, the scene shifts to the darkness of the tunnel as a train carries its passengers through.

## Piccadilly Line
Our journey continues at 17:15 at Finsbury Park. From the Victoria platform to Walthamstow Central, a train pulls a vast array of passengers through the city.

## Victoria Line
At 19:47, we encounter the signage for Blackhorse Road. A subway train sits stationary at the station, waiting for the signal to depart.

## Waterloo City Line
Finally, at 21:44, we arrive at Waterloo. Under the broad Underground signage, a man in a suit waits patiently for his train to arrive.
